title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise. An anagram is a word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of a different word or phrase, typically using all the original letters exactly once.

examples.explanation: Both strings contain the same characters with the same frequency.
The strings have different characters, so they are not anagrams.
Both strings contain the same characters: l, i, s, t, e, n.

constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters.
hints: Compare the frequency of each character in both strings.
If the lengths are different, they cannot be anagrams.
You can sort both strings and compare them directly.
Use a hash map or array to count character frequencies.
